news_summary: Today's news highlights meaningful reflection on Black European identity and ongoing commitment to human rights discussions at the upcoming EU summit.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité d'aujourd'hui met en lumière une réflexion approfondie sur l'identité des Noirs en Europe et un engagement constant en faveur des débats sur les droits de l'homme lors du prochain sommet européen.
news_summary_es: Las noticias de hoy ponen de relieve una reflexión profunda sobre la identidad negra europea y el compromiso constante con el debate sobre los derechos humanos en la próxima cumbre de la UE.

articles.title: Twenty years, one question: What does it mean to be Black and European?
articles.summary: For 20 years, British photographer Johny Pitts has been travelling around Europe with a camera and a question: what does it actually mean to be Black and European? His answer fills a room at the Maison Européenne de la Photographie in Paris. "Black Bricolage" brings together photographs, notebooks and documents from cities across the continent – Paris, Berlin, Lisbon, Marseille and Brussels – capturing the ordinary lives that rarely make the front page.
articles.content:
Twenty years, one question: What does it mean to be Black and European?

Culture

To display this content from YouTube, you must enable advertisement tracking and audience measurement. Accept Manage my choices One of your browser extensions seems to be blocking the video player from loading. To watch this content, you may need to disable it on this site. Try again arts24 © FRANCE 24 12:06

12:06 min Share

Play (12:06 min)

From the show

Reading time 1 min

For 20 years, British photographer Johny Pitts has been travelling around Europe with a camera and a question: what does it actually mean to be Black and European? His answer fills a room at the Maison Européenne de la Photographie in Paris. "Black Bricolage" brings together photographs, notebooks and documents from cities across the continent – Paris, Berlin, Lisbon, Marseille and Brussels – capturing the ordinary lives that rarely make the front page.
articles.source: News and stories on CULTURE, movies, music, literature - France 24
articles.reasoning: The article highlights a significant cultural and social exploration of Black European identity through a long-term photographic project, which promotes awareness, representation, and understanding of a marginalized community across multiple European cities. It has broad societal relevance and provides meaningful insight into human rights and social inclusion.
articles.personality_title: Photographer Johny Pitts explores Black European life in new Paris exhibit
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – For two decades, British photographer Johny Pitts has been asking a big question: what does it mean to be Black and European? This question guided his travels across many European cities.

**What happened** – Pitts collected photos, notebooks, and documents from places like Paris, Berlin, Lisbon, Marseille, and Brussels. These items show everyday moments in the lives of Black Europeans. Now, his work is on display in an exhibition called "Black Bricolage" at the Maison Européenne de la Photographie in Paris.

**Impact** – This exhibit shares stories that usually do not get much attention in the media. By showing ordinary life, it helps people understand the experiences of Black Europeans better. The project also supports social inclusion by giving a voice to a group often overlooked.

**What's next step** – The exhibition will allow more people to see and learn about Black European culture. It may inspire other artists and communities to explore identity and representation in new ways.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Johny Pitts’s 20-year project uses photography to reveal the everyday lives and identity of Black Europeans across major cities in Europe.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le photographe Johny Pitts explore la vie des Noirs européens dans une nouvelle exposition à Paris
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Depuis vingt ans, le photographe britannique Johny Pitts pose une grande question : que signifie être Noir et européen ? Cette question a guidé ses voyages dans plusieurs villes d'Europe.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Pitts a rassemblé des photos, des carnets et des documents de lieux comme Paris, Berlin, Lisbonne, Marseille et Bruxelles. Ces éléments montrent des moments de la vie quotidienne des Noirs européens. Aujourd’hui, son travail est exposé dans une exposition appelée « Black Bricolage » à la Maison Européenne de la Photographie à Paris.

**Impact** – Cette exposition partage des histoires qui ne sont pas souvent mises en avant dans les médias. En montrant la vie ordinaire, elle aide les gens à mieux comprendre l'expérience des Noirs européens. Le projet soutient aussi l'inclusion sociale en donnant une voix à un groupe souvent ignoré.

**Étape suivante** – L’exposition permettra à plus de personnes de découvrir et d’apprendre sur la culture des Noirs européens. Elle pourrait inspirer d’autres artistes et communautés à explorer l’identité et la représentation de nouvelles façons.

**Conclusion en une phrase** – Le projet de 20 ans de Johny Pitts utilise la photographie pour révéler la vie quotidienne et l’identité des Noirs européens dans les grandes villes d’Europe.
articles.personality_title_es: El fotógrafo Johny Pitts explora la vida de los negros europeos en una nueva exposición en París
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Durante veinte años, el fotógrafo británico Johny Pitts ha hecho una gran pregunta: ¿qué significa ser negro y europeo? Esta pregunta guió sus viajes por varias ciudades de Europa.

**Qué pasó** – Pitts reunió fotos, cuadernos y documentos de lugares como París, Berlín, Lisboa, Marsella y Bruselas. Estos muestran momentos de la vida cotidiana de los negros europeos. Ahora, su trabajo se presenta en una exposición llamada "Black Bricolage" en la Maison Européenne de la Photographie en París.

**Impacto** – Esta exposición comparte historias que normalmente no reciben mucha atención en los medios. Al mostrar la vida diaria, ayuda a las personas a entender mejor la experiencia de los negros europeos. El proyecto también apoya la inclusión social al dar voz a un grupo a menudo ignorado.

**Próximo paso** – La exposición permitirá que más personas vean y aprendan sobre la cultura de los negros europeos. Podría inspirar a otros artistas y comunidades a explorar la identidad y la representación de nuevas maneras.

**Resumen en una frase** – El proyecto de 20 años de Johny Pitts usa la fotografía para mostrar la vida cotidiana y la identidad de los negros europeos en ciudades importantes de Europa.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a diverse mosaic of intertwined European cityscapes—Parisian streets, Berlin walls, Lisbon rooftops, Marseille harbors, and Brussels squares—woven together with rich, textured patterns inspired by African textiles, all illuminated by soft, natural earth tones, symbolizing the vibrant, interconnected lives and cultures of Black Europeans across the continent.

articles.title: Press release - European Parliament press kit for the informal EU summit of 23-24 April 2026
articles.summary: This press kit includes a selection of EP press releases outlining key priorities regarding the issues on the agenda of the informal meeting of heads of state or government in Cyprus. Source : © European Union, 2026 - EP
articles.content:
Representing the European Parliament at the informal summit, President Roberta Metsola will address heads of state or government on 24 April.

At their meeting, the heads of state or government are expected to focus on the next EU long-term budget 2028-2034 (Multiannual Financial Framework). Leaders are also set to address the situation in the Middle East and Europe’s response to the subsequent geopolitical and economic fall-out. They will also discuss the ongoing war in Ukraine, and Europe’s ongoing efforts to secure a just and lasting peace, with President Zelenskyy.



Multiannual Financial Framework 2028 - 2034

On April 15, the Budget Committee adopted its draft interim report on the 2028-2034 EU Budget. The Committee is proposing a €1.78 trillion budget (2025 constant prices, €2.01 trillion in current prices) for the seven-year period, representing a nominal increase of €175.11 billion compared with the Commission’s July 2025 proposal (€197.30 billion in current prices). This means that the long-term budget would be set at 1.27% of EU GNI, with debt servicing for the NextGenerationEU recovery fund (0.11% of GNI) over and above the multiannual financial framework (MFF) ceilings.

MEPs want this increase of approximately 10% to be allocated to key EU programmes, with no extra money for administration or decentralised EU agencies. MEPs say their proposal is the minimum amount of money the EU needs to meet its commitments, respond to citizens’ expectations, and address the major challenges it faces.

Plenary will debate the next EU long-term budget on 28 April and vote on its negotiating position on 29 April. Once its position has been adopted, Parliament will be ready to start negotiations with the Council on the regulation setting the structure and main figures for the 2028-2034 budget. Talks can begin once the Council agrees on its common position. Approval of the regulation on the long-term budget requires Parliament’s consent.

On 14 April, the co-rapporteurs of the draft interim report Siegfried Mureşan (EPP, RO) and Carla Tavares (S&D, PT) held a press conference on Parliament's negotiation position.

Further reading

Vote in the Budget Committee on the EU long-term budget

Press Conference on Parliaments negotiation position

National and Regional Partnership Plans: statements by lead MEPs

The EU’s long-term budget explained



Middle East

On April 15, Parliament’s Foreign Affairs Committee discussed the ongoing events in Iran with Nobel Peace Prize laureate Shirin Ebadi and representatives from Iranian democratic parties and other democracy advocates. MEPs expressed their solidarity with the Iranian people and their concern for their safety. Watch the full debate here.

On 11 March, MEPs debated the US-Israeli military operation against Iran, its consequences, and the need to support Iranians. On the day the first US-Israeli strikes were launched on 28 February, Roberta Metsola said that the “events unfolding in Iran must not lead to a spiral of escalation that could threaten the Middle East, Europe and beyond".

At the opening of Parliament’s plenary session on 9 March, President Metsola reminded MEPs in a statement of the 47 years of brutal repression the Iranian people have suffered, and of Parliament’s proud history of standing with those people. Condemning Iran’s indiscriminate and desperate attacks on the Gulf States, she said Parliament would “continue to push for people in Iran to live in liberty and to choose their own destiny”.

On 12 February, MEPs adopted a resolution condemning the Iranian regime’s violence against its own people, in particular its targeting of civil society actors, protesters, women, minorities and certain communities. MEPs reaffirmed their solidarity with the Iranian people, underlining that Iranians are the sole legitimate source of sovereignty in Iran. They called on the Council and the Commission to expand targeted sanctions. The resolution is available here.

In a statement issued on 29 January, leading Foreign Affairs committee MEPs welcomed the Council’s confirmation of the designation of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation. Parliament had been calling for designating the IRGC as a terrorist organisation for a long time. The IRGC is now treated in the same way as other EU-listed terrorist organisations.

Further reading

Debate with Iranian opposition voices

Debate on the situation in the Middle East

Statement by European Parliament President Roberta Metsola at the opening of the March plenary session

David McAllister’s statement on avoiding war in the Middle East

Resolution on the human rights situations in Iran, Türkiye and Uganda

Leading MEPs welcome EU terror listing of Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps

Parliament condemns Iran’s brutal repression of protesters



Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine

On 24 February, Parliament held an extraordinary plenary session, marking four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy addressed MEPs during the session. Opening the sitting, European Parliament President Roberta Metsola said: “Ukraine's security is Europe’s security, Ukraine's freedom is Europe’s freedom, and Ukraine's future is in Europe.”

In a video message from Kyiv, Zelenskyy expressed gratitude for the European Parliament’s continued support for Ukraine, also pointing out the need for further support from the European Union. He stressed the need for credible security guarantees for Ukraine to prevent Russia from advancing its aggression in Europe. He called for strong sanctions, an end to Europe’s reliance on Russian energy, the shutting out of Russian banks from Europe, a clamp-down on sanctions evasion and Moscow’s shadow fleet, and refused entry to Europe for Russian war criminals.

Also on 24 February, the presidents of the European Parliament, the European Council and the European Commission issued a joint statement on the fourth anniversary of Russia's illegal invasion of Ukraine. They reiterated that the European Union stands firmly with Ukraine and its citizens and stressed that the EU will continue to provide comprehensive political, financial, economic, humanitarian, military and diplomatic support for Ukraine and its people. The three presidents also highlighted that “the future of a secure and prosperous Ukraine lies in the European Union. Ukraine can count on our full support on its EU accession and on its post-war reconstruction.”

On 11 February, MEPs approved a €90 billion EU loan to Ukraine to address urgent financing needs amid the ongoing war with Russia. Of the loan, €30 billion will be made available for macro-financial assistance or budget support, delivered through the EU’s Ukraine Facility. €60 billion will be allocated to strengthen Ukraine’s defence capabilities and support the procurement of military equipment, ensuring timely access to critical defence products. On Thursday 23 April, Council adopted the last piece of legislation allowing for the loan to be disbursed.

Further reading

Fourth anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine

Parliaments stands firm with Ukraine

Eastern Flank defence

Parliament approves support loan package
articles.reasoning: The article details significant EU parliamentary actions addressing major geopolitical challenges including support for Ukraine amid war, condemnation of human rights abuses in Iran, and efforts to secure peace and stability in Europe and the Middle East. These actions represent broad, meaningful political commitments with positive real-world impact on human rights, peace, and security for millions of people.
articles.personality_title: European Parliament sets priorities for EU budget and global challenges at 2026 summit
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context**
The European Parliament is preparing for an informal meeting of EU leaders in Cyprus on April 23-24, 2026. This summit focuses on important topics like the EU’s future budget, conflicts in the Middle East, and the ongoing war in Ukraine.

**What happened**
President Roberta Metsola will speak to heads of state and government on April 24. The Parliament’s Budget Committee recently proposed a €1.78 trillion budget for 2028-2034, aiming to increase funding for key programs without raising administrative costs. Parliament will vote on this budget in late April and then begin talks with the EU Council. Meanwhile, members of Parliament showed strong support for the Iranian people by condemning human rights abuses and welcoming the EU's designation of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard as a terrorist group. On Ukraine, Parliament marked four years of Russia’s invasion by approving a €90 billion loan to help Ukraine with finances and defense. President Zelenskyy thanked the EU for its ongoing support.

**Impact**
This summit highlights the EU’s firm commitment to addressing major challenges through clear plans and actions. Increasing the long-term budget shows readiness to invest in Europe’s future. Supporting Ukraine financially and militarily strengthens its defense against aggression. Speaking out against abuses in Iran promotes human rights and democracy. These steps affect millions by aiming to keep Europe stable, secure, and supportive of justice.

**What's next step**
After the April 28-29 vote, Parliament will negotiate the budget with the Council once the Council agrees on its position. The EU will continue to provide Ukraine with financial and military aid and maintain diplomatic pressure on Iran to protect human rights. The summit’s outcomes will guide the EU’s policies for the next several years.

**One-sentence takeaway**
The European Parliament is advancing a stronger EU budget and global support efforts to promote peace, security, and human rights at the 2026 summit.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le Parlement européen fixe ses priorités pour le budget de l'UE et les défis mondiaux lors du sommet 2026
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte**
Le Parlement européen se prépare pour une réunion informelle des dirigeants de l'UE à Chypre les 23 et 24 avril 2026. Ce sommet porte sur des sujets importants comme le futur budget de l'UE, les conflits au Moyen-Orient et la guerre en cours en Ukraine.

**Ce qui s’est passé**
La présidente Roberta Metsola s’adressera aux chefs d’État et de gouvernement le 24 avril. La commission du budget a récemment proposé un budget de 1,78 billion d’euros pour 2028-2034, visant à augmenter les fonds pour des programmes clés sans augmenter les coûts administratifs. Le Parlement votera ce budget fin avril puis entamera des négociations avec le Conseil de l’UE. Par ailleurs, les députés ont exprimé leur soutien au peuple iranien en condamnant les violations des droits humains et ont salué la désignation par l’UE du Corps des Gardiens de la Révolution iranien comme organisation terroriste. Concernant l’Ukraine, le Parlement a marqué les quatre ans de l’invasion russe en approuvant un prêt de 90 milliards d’euros pour aider financièrement et militairement l’Ukraine. Le président Zelenskyy a remercié l’UE pour son soutien continu.

**Impact**
Ce sommet montre l’engagement clair de l’UE à relever les grands défis avec des plans et des actions concrètes. L’augmentation du budget à long terme témoigne de la volonté d’investir dans l’avenir de l’Europe. Soutenir financièrement et militairement l’Ukraine renforce sa défense contre l’agression. S’exprimer contre les abus en Iran promeut les droits humains et la démocratie. Ces mesures touchent des millions de personnes en visant à maintenir la stabilité, la sécurité et la justice en Europe.

**Prochaine étape**
Après le vote des 28 et 29 avril, le Parlement négociera le budget avec le Conseil dès que celui-ci aura adopté sa position commune. L’UE continuera d’apporter une aide financière et militaire à l’Ukraine et maintiendra la pression diplomatique sur l’Iran pour protéger les droits humains. Les résultats du sommet orienteront les politiques de l’UE pour les prochaines années.

**Résumé en une phrase**
Le Parlement européen fait avancer un budget européen renforcé et des actions globales pour la paix, la sécurité et les droits humains lors du sommet 2026.
articles.personality_title_es: El Parlamento Europeo establece prioridades para el presupuesto de la UE y desafíos globales en la cumbre 2026
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto**
El Parlamento Europeo se prepara para una reunión informal de líderes de la UE en Chipre los días 23 y 24 de abril de 2026. Esta cumbre se centra en temas importantes como el futuro presupuesto de la UE, los conflictos en Oriente Medio y la guerra en curso en Ucrania.

**Qué pasó**
La presidenta Roberta Metsola hablará con los jefes de estado y gobierno el 24 de abril. El Comité de Presupuestos propuso recientemente un presupuesto de 1,78 billones de euros para 2028-2034, buscando aumentar fondos para programas clave sin subir los costos administrativos. El Parlamento votará este presupuesto a finales de abril y luego iniciará negociaciones con el Consejo de la UE. Además, los diputados expresaron su apoyo al pueblo iraní condenando abusos de derechos humanos y celebraron la designación del Cuerpo de la Guardia Revolucionaria de Irán como grupo terrorista por la UE. Sobre Ucrania, el Parlamento conmemoró cuatro años de la invasión rusa aprobando un préstamo de 90.000 millones de euros para ayudar financieramente y militarmente a Ucrania. El presidente Zelenskyy agradeció el apoyo continuo de la UE.

**Impacto**
Esta cumbre muestra el compromiso claro de la UE para enfrentar grandes desafíos con planes y acciones concretas. Aumentar el presupuesto a largo plazo refleja la voluntad de invertir en el futuro de Europa. Apoyar a Ucrania financieramente y militarmente fortalece su defensa contra la agresión. Hablar contra los abusos en Irán promueve los derechos humanos y la democracia. Estas medidas afectan a millones al buscar mantener la estabilidad, seguridad y justicia en Europa.

**Próximo paso**
Después de la votación del 28 y 29 de abril, el Parlamento negociará el presupuesto con el Consejo una vez que este acuerde su posición común. La UE continuará brindando ayuda financiera y militar a Ucrania y mantendrá la presión diplomática sobre Irán para proteger los derechos humanos. Los resultados de la cumbre guiarán las políticas de la UE durante los próximos años.

**Frase clave**
El Parlamento Europeo avanza en un presupuesto más fuerte y acciones globales para la paz, seguridad y derechos humanos en la cumbre 2026.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a grand round table shaped like the European continent, with glowing puzzle pieces symbolizing the EU budget fitting together, surrounded by peaceful doves carrying olive branches representing unity and hope, while a distant horizon shows a sturdy bridge connecting two lands symbolizing support and peace efforts between Europe and Ukraine.
